page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m2.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.25 GHz. The processing power is delivered by an AMD EPYC processor, configured with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading.

The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per CPU core. This 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio aligns the server with compute-focused workloads. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the dedicated AMD EPYC processing cores and the lower memory-to-CPU ratio, this instance is structured for compute-intensive tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. Suitable workloads include batch processing, web serving, microservices, and lightweight computational tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m2.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, running at a clock speed of 2.25 GHz. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It contains no local storage and relies on network-attached storage options, supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. With its dedicated CPU allocation and compute-leaning resource balance, this instance is designed for workloads such as web serving, microservices, and lightweight batch processing tasks that require consistent CPU performance without a large memory footprint.
